title: Multi-Actor Service Discovery and Conflicting Outcomes · Claim Audit: Service Journey
summary: Operators, end users, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users have different workflows, incentives, harms, and definitions of success. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 service journey increment in multi-actor service map and conflict register. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.
Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Service Journey · Theory Service Journey
steps.instruction:
theory case — End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Service Journey contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Service Journey · Theory Service Journey.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service. For Predict Service Journey · Theory Service Journey, keep Service Journey connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Service Journey condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

steps.instruction:
theory case — End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. content.text: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The worked example variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service. A defensible solution applies Service Journey, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-actor service map and conflict register.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A screen-level user flow captures the whole service. This fails the Service Journey contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 (CS2023 final report) — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.

steps.title: Defend the Service Journey model · Theory Service Journey
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Service Journey: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.
